w:r.w:t: Below is a **complete tasklist** for you to implement **Phase 1** and **Phase 2** for extending your semantic data catalog, following the current state of your database: currently, the catalog only has `product_types`, `products`, `variants`, `options`, `option_values`, `variant_options`, `media`, while the search side already has full projection/metadata.
The goal of this tasklist is:
* Make the `catalog` the **source of truth** for semantic data.
* so that the search function no longer has to "guess" facets from poor data.
* Upgrade the projection builder to build facets and search documents more accurately.
* Follow each phase to avoid disrupting the ongoing flow.
The most important, but minimal, addition:
Expanding the semantic layer more fully:
After Phase 1, your system should achieve the following:
* The product can be fitted with various skin types.
* retain the attribute from option values
* Search preview/admin search status is starting to become more meaningful.
I recommend splitting it into **2 migrations**.
## Migration P1.1 — master tables + product columns
is_active BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE NOT NULL,
created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,
is_active BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE NOT NULL,
created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,
is_active BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE NOT NULL,
created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,
is_active BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE NOT NULL,
created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,
is_active BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE NOT NULL,
created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,
is_highlighted BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE NOT NULL,
created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,
FOREIGN KEY (product_id) REFERENCES products(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
FOREIGN KEY (ingredient_id) REFERENCES ingredients(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,
FOREIGN KEY (product_id) REFERENCES products(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
FOREIGN KEY (skin_type_id) REFERENCES skin_types(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,
FOREIGN KEY (product_id) REFERENCES products(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
FOREIGN KEY (concern_id) REFERENCES concerns(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,
FOREIGN KEY (product_id) REFERENCES products(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
FOREIGN KEY (tag_id) REFERENCES tags(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
And relation model or aggregate support:
* [ ] Add repository/query support to load associations by `productId`
* [ ] product detail admin response must include:
Currently, `fetchProjectionSource(...)` is setting `brand_id`, `brand_name` = `NULL` and `replaceFacetValues(...)` is only building facets from option values.
* [ ] Keep the `attribute` facet from the option values as they are.
* [ ] ensures that when the product-brand relation changes, the enqueue projection rebuilds.
* [ ] Ensure that when product ingredients/concerns/skin types/tags change, enqueue rebuild
* [ ] unit test normalization for ingredient/tag text
* [ ] repository integration test for relation tables
* [ ] schema has enough master tables + relation tables
* [ ] admin CRUD for 5 master data to run
* [ ] projection rebuild obtains brand + semantic associations
* [ ] search query/facet reflects ingredients / concerns / skin types / tags
* [ ] seed data can test these cases
# PHASE 2 — A more complete Semantic layer
After Phase 2, the system needs to support the following additional components:
* Attribute-based facets are better than variant options.
I recommend splitting it into **3 migrations**.
is_active BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE NOT NULL,
created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,
is_active BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE NOT NULL,
created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,
created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,
FOREIGN KEY (product_id) REFERENCES products(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
FOREIGN KEY (benefit_id) REFERENCES benefits(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,
FOREIGN KEY (product_id) REFERENCES products(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
FOREIGN KEY (collection_id) REFERENCES collections(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
data_type VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL, -- TEXT, NUMBER, BOOLEAN
is_filterable BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE NOT NULL,
is_searchable BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE NOT NULL,
is_active BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE NOT NULL,
created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,
created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,
FOREIGN KEY (product_id) REFERENCES products(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
## Migration P2.3 — optional product/variant merch columns
If you want to start bringing some of your merchandising back to the source model:
ADD COLUMN is_featured BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE NOT NULL,
ADD COLUMN is_new BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE NOT NULL,
ADD COLUMN is_best_seller BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE NOT NULL;
ADD COLUMN is_default BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE NOT NULL,
* [ ] product admin detail response must include:
* Product attribute facet: also `attribute`, but `facet_label` clearly distinguishes them.
* [ ] Update the search query to include `facet_alignment_score` in the calculation:
* [ ] Added admin search preview to display indexed benefits/collections/attributes
* [ ] admin CRUD for benefits/collections/attributes to work
* [ ] product detail admin displays full semantic data
* [ ] search preview reveals new semantic data
* [ ] search query/facet accurately reflects those semantic data
#9. The actual order of execution I recommend
#10. Checklist to start working on right away
If you want to start today, the first order should be:
If you wish, the next step is for me to write the **complete SQL migration set for Phase 1** for you, ready to copy into Flyway.
